intro: Sarcoglottis schwackei, also known as Schwacke's sarcoglottis, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its extracts have been reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as potential anti-cancer and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of S. schwackei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, S. schwackei is commonly prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and is used to treat various ailments, including fever, respiratory infections, and skin conditions.
